changed_construction_audit.plain_language_claim: The label encoded a demonstrative-plus-head analysis without the overt classifier found in the relevant Cantonese forms. It had no constructor, accepted fixture, standardized case, or verified output, while the overt demonstrative-classifier-noun structure is already represented by OvertHeadDemonstrativeClassifierNP.

sources.citation: Bond, Francis and Joanna Ut-Seong Sio. 2024. A Construction-based Approach to Cantonese Classifiers.
sources.locator: Preserved source record in archived DemonstrativeHeadNP note; demonstrative-classifier-noun structure

sources.citation: Lam, Charles, Chaak Ming Lau, and Jackson L. Lee. 2024. Multi-Tiered Cantonese Word Segmentation.
sources.locator: Preserved source record in archived DemonstrativeHeadNP note; classifier segmentation evidence

sources.citation: Canto Span constructor and output audit

implementation_validation: PASS required before handoff: 545 regression cases, 43 NP cases, 1,194 construction assertions, 168 note/test alignment, two direct implementation probes, one compatibility-alias probe, nominal-wrapper audit, historical reachability audits, panel workflow, promotion and release gates, documentation links, and repository verification.
linguistic_confidence: No retained construction gained linguistic evidence or status. All three probes have evidence weight zero. DemonstrativeHeadNP was retired as a constructorless known misanalysis; its sources and history remain preserved.
overclaim_review_scope: Inspected constructor definitions, complete diagnostic rows, public compatibility aliases, and current notes for four nominal labels. Distinguished direct internal output from public alias exposure, recorded PossessiveClassifierNP as nested-only reachability, and retired only the constructorless label whose own source record contradicted its analysis.
unresolved_release_questions: Twenty-nine retained labels still have no direct standardized case.
PossessiveClassifierNP is currently observed only as a nested wrapper inside bounded result frames, not as a standalone possessive parser.
Comment and PerfectiveResultPredicate remain shadowed or unobserved in complete output.
The full dormant-label review remains due by handoff sequence 23.
RUL and PFV survey integration remains paused while collection proceeds.
